segments.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:
Would a wise person answer with empty notions or fill their belly with the hot east wind?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Would they argue with useless words, with speeches that have no value?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: But you even undermine piety and hinder devotion to God.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Your sin prompts your mouth; you adopt the tongue of the crafty.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Your own mouth condemns you, not mine; your own lips testify against you.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Are you the first man ever born? Were you brought forth before the hills?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Do you listen in on God's council? Do you have a monopoly on wisdom?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: What do you know that we do not know? What insights do you have that we do not have?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: The gray-haired and the aged are on our side, men even older than your father.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Are God's consolations not enough for you, words spoken gently to you?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Why has your heart carried you away, and why do your eyes flash,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: so that you vent your rage against God and pour out such words from your mouth?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: What are mortals, that they could be pure, or those born of woman, that they could be righteous?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: If God places no trust in his holy ones, if even the heavens are not pure in his eyes,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: how much less mortals, who are vile and corrupt, who drink up evil like water!
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Listen to me and I will explain to you; let me tell you what I have seen,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: what the wise have declared, hiding nothing received from their ancestors
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: (to whom alone the land was given when no foreigners moved among them):
All his days the wicked man suffers torment, the ruthless man through all the years stored up for him.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Terrifying sounds fill his ears; when all seems well, marauders attack him.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: He despairs of escaping the realm of darkness; he is marked for the sword.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: He wanders about for food like a vulture; he knows the day of darkness is at hand.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Distress and anguish fill him with terror; troubles overwhelm him, like a king poised to attack,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: because he shakes his fist at God and vaunts himself against the Almighty,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: defiantly charging against him with a thick, strong shield.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Though his face is covered with fat and his waist bulges with flesh,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: he will inhabit ruined towns and houses where no one lives, houses crumbling to rubble.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: He will no longer be rich and his wealth will not endure, nor will his possessions spread over the land.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: He will not escape the darkness; a flame will wither his shoots, and the breath of God's mouth will carry him away.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Let him not deceive himself by trusting what is worthless, for he will get nothing in return.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Before his time he will wither, and his branches will not flourish.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: He will be like a vine stripped of its unripe grapes, like an olive tree shedding its blossoms.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: For the company of the godless will be barren, and fire will consume the tents of those who love bribes.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: They conceive trouble and give birth to evil; their womb fashions deceit.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz
